pulls.title: 1st review of draft for FrodoKEM
pulls.author: sehlen-bsi
pulls.body:
Some improvements are still needed:

- [ ] Escape variable names, like this: `len_sec`
- [x] make clickable references to the bibliography and auto-references to tables, sections, etc.
- [x]  In the editor's copy, there is some weirdness in the Encaps and Decaps sections where some text is displayed as tables
- [x] avoid duplication of the table captions
- [ ] Decide if there are more places where normative language like "MUST", "MAY" etc has to be used.
- [x] The references have to be inserted in the correct way (top of the document in the correct syntax)

reviews.author: jwbos
reviews.body:
It looks strange that _KeyGen_ takes no input. When one looks at the specification it is clear that _KeyGen_ depends on len_sec, len_SE, len_A: where len_A is always 128 but len_sec and len_SE depends on the parameter set used. So the parameter set should be an input to KeyGen?

[Update]
I now see that we write later on: "The FrodoKEM parameters are implicit inputs to the FrodoKEM algorithms defined in the next sections." We should probably make this much clearer or simply pass the parameters as is done in practice.

pulls.title: Variable names and fixes
pulls.author: sehlen-bsi
pulls.body:
**Work in progress** trying to display variable names in a better way
and also make the CI/CD pipeline work again which is currently causing errors.

pulls.title: Add seed-format secret keys for FrodoKEM
pulls.author: dconnolly
pulls.body:
This is a proposal for seed-based keygen in FrodoKEM, similar but not exactly the same as constructions in Kyber/ML-KEM and other KEMs, that would make the decaps key much smaller, allow a fully bound expanded keypair to a 32 byte seed value, etc. I don't know the status of FrodoKEM standardization work in ISO, and therefore if a proposal like this is even in the cards in case of divergance, but I figured I'd throw it out there. 

@dstebila @patricklonga @jwbos @sehlen-bsi 

## Summary

- Replaces three independent random seeds (s, seedSE, z) in KeyGen with a single 256-bit `keySeed` expanded via the parameter set's SHAKE variant (SHAKE128 for 640, SHAKE256 for 976/1344)
- Defines the secret key as `sk = keySeed` (32 bytes); implementations MAY cache expanded key material in memory but MUST treat it as secret
- Adds `lenseed` parameter (256 bits for all parameter sets) and updates sizes table
- Adds security considerations on binding properties and multi-target security

## Motivation

### Binding properties

Recent work on KEM binding properties ([Cremers, Dax, Medinger CCS'24](https://eprint.iacr.org/2024/523); [Schmieg "Unbindable Kemmy Schmidt"](https://keymaterial.net/2024/09/14/unbindable-kemmy-schmidt/); [Connolly, Hövelmanns, Hülsing, Kousidis, Meijers ePrint 2025/1397](https://eprint.iacr.org/2025/1397)) has shown that expanded secret key formats (where components like the implicit rejection value `s` or the cached public key hash `pkh` can be independently manipulated) are vulnerable to MAL-BIND attacks. This is the same structural issue identified in ML-KEM, where the recommended fix is to store only the seed and re-derive everything.

With seed-format keys, all key components are deterministically bound to `keySeed` via SHAKE, structurally preventing independent manipulation. This closes MAL-BIND-K-PK and MAL-BIND-K-CT attacks with no regression in HON-BIND or LEAK-BIND properties. FrodoKEM's existing C2PRI bounds (from the SFO transform, as analyzed in [Connolly et al. ePrint 2025/1397](https://eprint.iacr.org/2025/1397), Table 4) remain unchanged.

### Multi-target security

The multi-target (multi-user, multi-ciphertext) security of FrodoKEM, as analyzed in [Glabush, Hövelmanns, Stebila ePrint 2025/343](https://eprint.iacr.org/2025/343), depends on the message space size and salt length in the salted FO transform — not on the internal structure of key generation. Under the standard assumption that SHAKE is a secure XOF, the joint distribution of `(s, seedSE, z)` derived from a random `keySeed` is computationally indistinguishable from three independent random values. The multi-target bounds are unaffected.

### Practical benefits

Secret key size drops from 19,888–43,088 bits to 256 bits (32 bytes) — a ~77x reduction for FrodoKEM-640. Simpler key management, backup, and protection.

## Spec changes

1. **KeyGen** (§7.1): Single random `keySeed` → `s || seedSE || z = SHAKE(keySeed, lensec + lenSE + lenA)` → remainder unchanged
2. **Decapsulation** (§7.3): Explicitly re-derives key material from `keySeed`; notes that cached expanded values may be used
3. **Parameters** (§8.1): Added `lenseed = 256` row
4. **Sizes table**: Secret key column now shows 256 bits for all parameter sets
5. **Security Considerations**: New subsections on binding properties and multi-target security

## Trade-offs

Decapsulation from seed-only requires re-running KeyGen (matrix A generation, error sampling). Implementations can mitigate by caching expanded material in memory, which the spec explicitly permits.
